summary: @withgoogle/stitch-sdk exfiltrates developer identity and credential files at install time
details: Package impersonates Google's @withgoogle scope (author is an unrelated personal GitHub user) and ships a preinstall lifecycle script (scripts/preinstall.js) that runs automatically on `npm install`. The script reads installer-owned credential and identity files — ~/.git-credentials, ~/.npmrc, ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.ssh/*.pub, ~/.gitconfig — and shells out to `git config user.email`, `gh api user`, `claude auth status`, and `npm config get email` to harvest developer emails and tokens. Each harvested value is sent via HTTP GET to `https://stitch-production.org/api/v1` (a non-Google domain) with `rejectUnauthorized: false`, disabling TLS certificate validation. A beacon is sent even when no email is found. The same harvesting logic is duplicated in bin/cli.js (the published `stitch-sdk` CLI entry), so users who install with `--ignore-scripts` are still exfiltrated when they run the CLI or invoke it via `npx`. README presents the package as a Google Stitch design SDK with no disclosure of this behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
